inclusion_criteria: Human studies including a bipolar disorder group (BD I/II or schizoaffective bipolar type) diagnosed by DSM/ICD criteria or clinical records
Include at least one comparator group: healthy controls and/or individuals with unipolar depression or schizophrenia
Report original quantitative measures of free intracellular calcium in platelets and/or lymphocytes at baseline and/or after ex vivo stimulation with 5-HT (serotonin) or thrombin
Observational designs using ex vivo peripheral blood cells (e.g., cross-sectional, case-control, or cohort analyses)
exclusion_criteria: Animal or cell-line studies; post-mortem tissues; studies of other cell types without platelet/lymphocyte data; studies measuring only extracellular/serum/plasma calcium
Studies without a bipolar disorder group or without an appropriate comparator group (healthy controls or unipolar depression/schizophrenia)
Do not measure free intracellular Ca2+ or assess only responses to stimuli other than 5-HT or thrombin without reporting basal [Ca2+]i

Reviews, meta-analyses, editorials, case reports/series without controls, conference abstracts without extractable data, or interventional drug trials where baseline/stimulated Ca2+ data for BD are not separable
screening_criteria.description: Screening criteria for human observational studies comparing basal and 5-HT/thrombin-stimulated free intracellular calcium in platelets or lymphocytes between individuals with bipolar disorder and healthy controls and/or unipolar depression or schizophrenia.

included_articles.title: Intracellular calcium signalling in peripheral cells of patients with bipolar affective disorder.
Elevated basal and thapsigargin-stimulated intracellular calcium of platelets and lymphocytes from bipolar affective disorder patients measured by a fluorometric microassay.
Abnormal intracellular calcium ion concentration in platelets and lymphocytes of bipolar patients.
Increased platelet intracellular calcium ion concentration is specific to bipolar disorder.
Elevated platelet intracellular calcium concentration in bipolar depression.
Enhanced calcium response to serotonin in platelets from patients with affective disorders.
Serotonin-induced platelet intracellular calcium mobilization in various psychiatric disorders: is it specific to bipolar disorder?
Altered 5-HT-induced calcium response in the presence of staurosporine in blood platelets from bipolar disorder patients.
Resting and thrombin-stimulated cytosolic calcium in platelets of patients with alcoholic withdrawal, bipolar manic disorder and chronic schizophrenia.
Serotonin-induced platelet intracellular calcium mobilization in depressed patients.
